title: Bunuelos
subtitle: Mexican fried dough discs with cinnamon sugar
excerpt: Mexican bunuelos are large, thin fried discs of dough dusted with cinnamon sugar. The dough is worked until very pliable, then rested and stretched as thin as possible before frying. They shatter when broken. Made across Latin America for Christmas and New Year celebrations, eaten plain or with a warm piloncillo syrup.
sourceNotes: Bunuelos (also written as bunyols or beignets in other regions) are a broad family of fried dough made across Spain, Latin America, and parts of Africa and the Middle East. The Mexican version, most closely associated with Christmas and New Year celebrations, is a thin, crisp, large disc fried golden and dusted with cinnamon sugar. Variations exist with anise seed in the dough. The recipe follows the central Mexican domestic tradition.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best made and eaten on the same day. The dough can be made and rested 1 hour ahead. Fry close to serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: deep-fried at 180 degrees C; the thin disc cooks in under 1 minute per side

glossaryTerms.definition: Mexican bunuelo dough should be stretched over the backs of your knuckles like pizza dough rather than rolled, which allows it to get thinner without tearing. Drape a rested dough ball over one fist and rotate it slowly, letting gravity and gentle knuckle stretching thin it to near-translucency. This takes practice; rolling with a pin is an acceptable shortcut, though the result is slightly less thin and may not blister as much.

content.text: The thinner the disc, the crisper the bunuelo. The
gets the dough thinner than a rolling pin can. Either way, rest the dough fully before attempting to thin it and work each ball immediately before frying.

Combine 300 g flour, 1 tsp baking powder, 4 g salt, and 1 tbsp caster sugar. Add 1 egg, 30 g melted butter, and 120 ml warm milk. Mix to a soft, pliable dough. Knead for 5 minutes until very smooth and elastic. Divide into 12 equal balls. Cover with a clean cloth and rest for 30 minutes.

Heat 1 litre vegetable oil to 180 degrees C. Mix 100 g granulated sugar with 2 tsp ground cinnamon in a shallow bowl. Take one dough ball and stretch or roll on a floured surface to a very thin disc, roughly 25 cm in diameter. Fry immediately in the hot oil for about 30 to 45 seconds per side until pale golden and blistered. Remove with a spider strainer and dust both sides immediately with the cinnamon sugar. Continue with remaining dough balls, stretching and frying one at a time.

items.problem: Dough shrinks back when rolling and will not stay thin
items.solution: The dough did not rest long enough. The gluten is tight and springs back. Rest for the full 30 minutes, or longer if the kitchen is cold.

items.problem: Bunuelo is pale and greasy rather than blistered and golden
items.solution: The oil was not hot enough. Maintain 180 degrees C between batches. Check with a thermometer before each disc goes in.
